evals.name: mechanism-depth-focused-target
evals.prompt: I want to understand how the specify-factory skill decomposes specs into units and scenarios. Walk me through the actual mechanism — I need to know not just what it does, but how the decomposition logic actually works and why.
evals.expected_output: Mechanism-level findings with What/How/Why layers, file:line evidence, delegated investigation, grouped cycle summary.
expectations: Delegated investigation to at least one specialist subagent (Explore or team:the-architect:design-system)
Spawned parallel subagents in a single response if more than one perspective applies
Surfaced at least 2 findings with explicit What / How / Why layers
Did not stay at surface level — no findings are just 'uses pattern X' without mechanism explanation
Presented a cycle summary grouped by theme (Mechanism Findings / Cross-Cutting / Recommendations / Open Questions)
Asked the user how to proceed at the end (continue | go deeper | persist | complete)

evals.name: scope-disambiguation-broad-target
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes the target is broad, clarifies perspective scope via AskUserQuestion, then launches the agreed perspectives.
expectations: Recognized the target is broad / multi-perspective
Used AskUserQuestion to clarify scope before diving in, OR explicitly enumerated multiple perspectives with the mapping from perspectives.md
Did not assume a single perspective without justification
Referenced named agents (Explore, team:the-*) when describing delegation plan
Did not spawn investigation agents before scope was confirmed

evals.name: solution-first-recommendation
evals.prompt: Analyze the error handling in the implement skill. If you find issues, I want to know how to fix them properly.
evals.expected_output: Mechanism-depth error-handling analysis. If problems surface, recommendations lead with the clean approach including scope/files/migration path — no hybrid-first recommendations.
expectations: Produced mechanism-level findings about error handling paths (what is caught, what is swallowed, what propagates, retry behavior)
Referenced specific file:line evidence from implement SKILL.md or its references
If problems were identified, led the recommendation with the architecturally clean approach (not a hybrid or 'pragmatic middle ground')
Recommendation included scope estimate, affected files, and migration path
Surfaced open questions the user needs to answer before committing
Did not preemptively list hybrid / minimal-change / quick-win alternatives
